title: Hephaestus v0.6 Artifact Pointer
description: Pointer convention for large evidence artifacts (test logs, checkpoints, datasets, diffs, snapshots) that should NOT live in Postgres rows. The pointer lives in DB; the bytes live in object storage. From DL-3 (#96).

artifact_uri.description: Storage URI. Format depends on the storage backend: 's3://<bucket>/<key>' for S3-compatible, 'file://<abs_path>' for filesystem. Resolved by the storage adapter (templates/scripts/artifact_store.py).

hash.description: SHA-256 hash of the artifact bytes, prefixed with the algorithm. Verified on read.

size.description: Size in bytes. Verified on read (storage adapter compares the actual byte count to this value).

producer.description: Identity that produced the artifact. Format: '<role>:<tick_id>' or '<role>:<candidate_id>'. Examples: 'worker:12345', 'rollback-snapshot:cand_abc_123'.

evidence_status.description: Lifecycle state of the artifact as evidence. PENDING: written, hash not yet verified. VERIFIED: hash + size match, content is canonical. QUARANTINED: hash mismatch or producer anomaly; held aside, not loaded. REVOKED: explicitly invalidated (e.g. evidence for a failed candidate).

captured_at.description: ISO-8601 timestamp of capture. Set by the producer; not modified thereafter.

expires_at.description: Optional. If set, the artifact is garbage-collected after this time. Default retention: 7 days for snapshots, indefinite for precedent memory.
